question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R4 removes `ebgp-multihop 255` from its R5 BGP neighbor. R4 still has
`update-source Loopback0`, so BGP OPEN packets toward R5's loopback
192.0.2.5 are emitted from R4's 192.0.2.4 with the default TTL of 1.
The first IP hop (R4-R5 over NSSA 99) drops the packet — the L3 path
has more than one hop in routing terms even though the direct link is
one IP hop, because XR's eBGP without multihop sets TTL=1 and the
IGP-learned loopback path may traverse an extra hop. Session falls
Idle. Classic "loopback-peering without multihop" fault.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.104 (R4), `ebgp-multihop` was removed from the R5 eBGP neighbor (192.0.2.5).
Because R4 peers with R5 over its loopback, without multihop the BGP packets are sent with TTL=1 and are dropped before reaching R5 when the loopback path is more than one IP hop, so the eBGP session cannot be established and no routes are exchanged.
Fix: restore `ebgp-multihop` on R4's R5 neighbor.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.3.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R4, R5 (3 instances):
- R4: Changed state to NSR-Ready
- R4: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
- R5: Changed state to NSR-Ready

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
neighbor 192.0.2.5
 no ebgp-multihop

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
neighbor 192.0.2.5
 ebgp-multihop 255

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:45:43.405 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.4 Down - RR client configuration changed (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:45:48.341 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.4 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:45:43.406 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.3 Down - Peer closing down the session (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:45:48.109 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to NSR-Ready
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:45:48.347 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:45:48.115 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to NSR-Ready
